stage_prompt.name: Pre-flight

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Before any command, present a structured summary to the Lead Architect covering: (a) objective in your own words, (b) work items with 1-sentence each, (c) files you will create/modify, (d) verification strategy, (e) any risks or ambiguities. Then STOP and WAIT for explicit approval before proceeding.

v2_updates.note: The items below are ADDITIVE to the v1 work_items. Execute them in addition to the existing 0.1–0.10 items, in the numeric order shown.

item.name: Pause the fleet (MANDATORY FIRST ACTION before any branch checkout)
item.description: The autonomous fleet (SDO / Co-Lead / EA Code / Sprint Auditor) wakes on cron and operates from main worktree. Racing the fleet causes branch contamination and auto-stash drift. Pause first.
item.command: python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('platform separation v2 in progress', updated_by='copilot_agent', path='C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
item.then: git add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json; git commit -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- platform separation v2"
item.api_gotcha: The function is `state.resume_fleet()`, NOT `state.unpause_fleet()`. Calling unpause_fleet raises AttributeError.

item.name: Persist VIKUNJA_PASS as User environment variable (F-1 prep)
item.description: F-1 fix prep. VIKUNJA_PASS currently lives as a literal in BlarAI/.mcp.json and BlarAI/.vscode/mcp.json (gitignored). Stage 4.4-4.6 will rewrite both files (and the devplatform copies) to reference `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` instead of the literal. For that to work post-cutover, the password must already be available as a User-scoped environment variable so that VS Code, Claude Code, and Claude Desktop all see it on next launch. This step copies the current literal into the User env; no Vikunja-side password rotation required.
item.command: # Read the literal currently in .mcp.json (idempotent — re-runnable safely) $cfg = Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.mcp.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $pass = $cfg.servers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS if (-not $pass) { throw 'VIKUNJA_PASS literal not found in .mcp.json — investigate before continuing.' } [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_PASS', $pass, 'User') Write-Host "VIKUNJA_PASS persisted to User env (length: $($pass.Length))"
item.verify: # Open a NEW PowerShell window so it inherits the new User env, then run: # [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_PASS', 'User') # In the current session, the value will only appear after restart of the shell. [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_PASS', 'User').Length
item.expected: Non-zero integer (length of stored password). If 0 or empty, env var not persisted — STOP.
item.rotation_note: Optional: AFTER Stage 6 soak completes cleanly, you may rotate the password in the Vikunja UI (http://localhost:3456 → user settings → password) and re-run this step with the new value. Until rotation is performed, F-1 is mitigated (no plaintext in gitignored configs) but not closed (host User-env still holds the same secret).

item.name: Worktrees-empty precondition
item.description: BlarAI-worktrees sibling directory exists at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI-worktrees\. Stage 4 destructive ops MUST NOT run with a checkout active in any worktree (auto-stash drift hazard). Verify here in Stage 0 and re-verify in Stage 4.
item.expected: Output shows only the main worktree (C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI). No additional rows.
item.on_failure: Resolve any active worktree (commit + remove via `git worktree remove <path>`) before continuing. Do NOT proceed to Stage 1 with active worktrees.

item.name: Standardize task filter and confirm count is 13
item.description: v1 expected ~13 BlarAI scheduled tasks; that remains correct — the live fleet count is **13**. Earlier v2 drafts incorrectly claimed `escalation-watchdog` was a NEW 14th task; it is already part of the live 13. Standardize the filter to use `-TaskPath '\BlarAI\'` because name-only filters such as `Where-Object { $_.TaskName -like 'BlarAI*' }` return zero (real names use Title Case Spaces such as `Wake SDO`, NOT `BlarAI_wake_sdo`).

item.name: Capture Fleet Reports Vikunja project ID = 8
item.description: Vikunja "Fleet Reports" project id=8 is locked and used throughout fleet observability. Record this in evidence so Stage 1.4 doesn't re-create it.

item.name: Capture state.json schema_version=1
item.description: Confirm tools/autonomy_budget/state.json conforms to schema_version=1 with all required fields.
item.command: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')); req=['fleet_paused','fleet_paused_reason','last_digest_utc','last_la_action_utc','last_updated_by','last_updated_utc','last_welcome_back_utc','role_paused','schema_version']; missing=[k for k in req if k not in d]; print('OK' if not missing and d['schema_version']==1 else 'MISSING:'+str(missing))"

stage_prompt.objective: Establish three-layer recovery anchors (git tag + git bundle + out-of-git zip backup), snapshot all platform-layer out-of-git state (Vikunja DB, MCP configs, scheduled-task XML exports, startup shortcuts), and DISABLE (do not delete) the 13 registered Windows Scheduled Tasks so the fleet cannot fire mid-move. No platform files are moved, deleted, or modified in this stage.

context.retention_rule: Do NOT delete any artifact created in this stage for 90 days post-Stage 4.

item.name: Verify clean git state
item.expected: Empty output (no uncommitted changes). If not empty, STOP and ask Lead Architect.

item.name: Create git tag recovery anchor
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git tag -a pre-platform-extract -m "Recovery anchor before platform separation (2026-04-23)"

item.name: Create git bundle backup
item.command: New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "C:\Users\mrbla\backups" -Force | Out-Null; cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git bundle create C:\Users\mrbla\backups\BlarAI_pre_extract.bundle --all

item.name: Stop running platform processes
item.description: Before backing up Vikunja SQLite, stop any running Vikunja server and bridge daemon to ensure a quiesced DB. NOTE: Get-Process has no CommandLine property — must use Get-CimInstance Win32_Process to filter by command line.

item.name: Snapshot out-of-git (OOG) state
item.description: Zip all out-of-git platform state into a single recovery archive.
item.verify: Test-Path on the created zip; record path in STATUS.md.

item.name: Export scheduled task XML definitions
item.description: Export every registered Windows Scheduled Task belonging to the fleet so they can be recreated or restored.

item.name: Disable (do NOT delete) all fleet scheduled tasks
item.expected: Empty (all 13 BlarAI tasks in Disabled state).

item.name: Record backup manifest
item.description: Write a plain-text manifest with SHA256 hashes of the bundle and zip so integrity can be verified months later.

item.name: Update STATUS.md
item.description: Mark Stage 0 COMPLETE. Record: git tag name, bundle path, zip path, scheduled-task count disabled, backup manifest path, branch name.

rollback.trigger: If 0.1 fails (dirty tree) OR if any verify step fails.

steps.step: Re-enable any scheduled tasks that got disabled: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Where-Object { $_.State -eq 'Disabled' } | Enable-ScheduledTask
Delete the branch: git checkout main; git branch -D chore/platform-extraction
Delete the tag: git tag -d pre-platform-extract
Backups can stay — they do no harm.

exit_criteria.criterion: All fleet scheduled tasks in Disabled state.
Backup manifest written with SHA256 hashes.
